q: In a circular motion, the object just moves in a ___.
Rotation of earth about its own axis creates the cycle of ___ and night.
A ___ body is a body that does not deform or change shape.
The distance between any two ___ within a rigid body remains constant.
If the angular velocity of an object changes at a constant rate, the ___ is constant.
The motion is called the rotational motion under ___ angular acceleration if the rate of change of angular velocity is constant.
Rotational variables include angular displacement, angular velocity, and ___.
The kinematic equations for rotational motion are ___ to equations for constant acceleration in linear motion.
The average angular speed is expressed by the equation $\bar{\omega} = \frac{\omega + ___}{2}$.
The equation $\omega = \omega_0 + \alpha t$ relates angular velocity, initial angular velocity, angular acceleration, and ___.
The symbol for angular displacement is ___.
Angular acceleration is denoted by the Greek letter ___.
